title: Utility Classes Should Not Have Public Constructors
description: Utility classes, which only contain static members, shouldn't be instantiated. This rule flags any utility classes that have public constructors. Classes containing a 'public static void main(String[] args)' method are exceptions and are not considered as utility classes by this rule.
recommendation: Restrict the accessibility of the constructor for utility classes or make them private if possible.
impact: Preventing the instantiation of utility classes enhances code readability and maintainability. It also helps in avoiding unnecessary object creation.
